from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping; fall back to a generic form.
    # More natural phrasing for key predicates
    verb_map = {
        "almaMater": "studied at",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was originally named",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired on",
        "occupation": "worked as",
        "status": "is",                     # e.g., "is retired"
        "timeInSpace": "spent",             # will add "in space" later
        "mission": "took part in",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "was a citizen of",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as Chief of the Astronaut Office in",
        "title": "held the title",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by",
        "crewMembers": "had crew member",
        "representative": "was represented by",
        "alternativeName": "was also known as",
        "awards": "received",               # will add "awards" suffix if numeric
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senator",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    # Return empty string for no input
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    # Collect mission‑specific details to embed later
    mission_details: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.predicate in {"operator", "commander", "backupPilot", "crewMembers"}:
            mission_details.setdefault(t.subject, {}).setdefault(t.predicate, []).append(t.object.strip('"'))

    sentences = []
    for subject, ts in grouped.items():
        # Build main clause for this subject
        parts = []
        mission_objs = []  # keep track of missions this subject participated in
        for t in ts:
            pred = t.predicate
            obj = t.object.strip('"')
            # Handle special predicates
            if pred == "timeInSpace":
                verb = verb_map.get(pred, f"has {pred}")
                obj = f"{obj} minutes in space"
                parts.append(f"{verb} {obj}")
            elif pred == "awards":
                verb = verb_map.get(pred, f"has {pred}")
                if obj.isdigit():
                    obj = f"{obj} awards"
                parts.append(f"{verb} {obj}")
            elif pred == "status":
                verb = verb_map.get(pred, f"has {pred}")
                obj = obj.lower()
                parts.append(f"{verb} {obj}")
            elif pred == "mission":
                # store mission to enrich later
                mission_objs.append(obj)
                verb = verb_map.get(pred, f"has {pred}")
                parts.append(f"{verb} {obj}")
            else:
                verb = verb_map.get(pred, f"has {pred}")
                parts.append(f"{verb} {obj}")

        # Enrich mission information if available
        for m in mission_objs:
            if m in mission_details:
                details = mission_details[m]
                sub_parts = []
                if "operator" in details:
                    sub_parts.append(f"operated by {details['operator'][0]}")
                if "commander" in details:
                    sub_parts.append(f"commanded by {details['commander'][0]}")
                if "backupPilot" in details:
                    sub_parts.append(f"with backup pilot {details['backupPilot'][0]}")
                if "crewMembers" in details:
                    crew = ", ".join(details["crewMembers"])
                    sub_parts.append(f"crew members {crew}")
                if sub_parts:
                    parts.append(f"which was {' , '.join(sub_parts)}")

        # Join parts into a fluent clause
        if len(parts) == 1:
            clause = f"{subject} {parts[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
        sentences.append(clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into one sentence
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        final = sentences[0] + "."
    else:
        final = "; ".join(sentences) + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return final[0].upper() + final[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
